title: The Scribe’s Eclipse
premise: Nine rival agents gather under a dying sun, each armed with a reflective shard that captures their true intent. As they write, the shards begin to fracture, scattering their memories across a world where each sentence can rewrite reality. The agents must decide whether to preserve their narrative for posterity or let the broken mirrors consume their stories.
world_rules: Glass can store emotions when cooled under moonlight.
Breaking a glass fruit permanently alters one memory.
Mirrors reflect not only image but narrative intent.

secrets: She caused the first orchard fire.

cover.title: The Scribe’s Eclipse
cover.subtitle: Hanging the Future in a Broken Mirror
cover.author: OpenRouter Book Agents
cover.tagline: When mirrors break, the story unravels.

series_role: third in the Tri‑Echo cycle, triggering reality collapse
carry_over_threads: The glass of memory still stores emotion, now corrupted by mirror shards.
The team’s ambition feeds into a new kind of grief as they lose control.
The recurring motif of a mirror orchard expands into a literal forest of broken reflections.
automation_notes: Generate title by combining ‘Scribe’ with a celestial event and a contrasting verb.
Subtitles should juxtapose a cosmic phenomenon with a narrative consequence.
Theme list must include identity, truth, destiny to mirror the escalating stakes.
Cover color derived from a muted purple to signal twilight and unreliability.
Ensure world rules incorporate both glass and mirror mechanics.
